MCQs.question: What is the return type of a function that doesn't return anything?
What does the '?' symbol mean in function parameters?
MCQs.codeSnippet: function greet(name: string, greeting?: string): string { }

options.text: The parameter has a default value

MCQs.question: What is the key difference between interfaces and type aliases?

options.text: Interfaces can only define objects, type aliases can define any type
Interfaces support declaration merging, type aliases don't
Type aliases are faster at runtime

MCQs.question: What does the union type 'string | number' mean?

options.text: The value must be both a string and a number
The value can be either a string or a number
The value is converted from string to number
The value is always a string

MCQs.question: What does the intersection type 'Person & Employee' create?

options.text: A type that can be either Person or Employee
A type that has all properties from both Person and Employee
A type with only shared properties

MCQs.question: What is the value of Direction.Down in this enum?
When should you use type assertions?

options.text: When you know more about a type than TypeScript can infer
Makes all properties of User optional
Makes all properties of User required
A User type without name and email
A type with only name and email properties from User
A User type with optional name and email
An array of name and email values
